title: urticastrum evitatum
intro: Urticastrum evitatum, also known as Urtica dioica var. holosericea, is a subspecies of the stinging nettle plant. It is characterized by its tall stems, serrated leaves, and the presence of stinging hairs that contain formic acid, which can cause skin irritation. The medicinal properties of Urticastrum evitatum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and urinary tract infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs for enhanced health benefits.
